interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a hump, with the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeding the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 15Y (12.68%) peaks above 20Y (11.65%). The 2Y bulge reflects acute near-term inflation expectations and tight banking sector liquidity as BB maintains a restrictive repo rate at 10%. The 364D dip below 182D signals segmented demand in T-bill auctions. The 130bps 10Y-91D spread suggests compressed term premium amid persistent inflationary pressures and fiscal borrowing concerns.
implications: Elevated 2Y yields signal market expectation that BB will maintain or tighten policy rates longer, with inflation remaining above target in the near term
Banking sector liquidity stress is concentrated in the 1-2Y segment, likely driven by AD ratio pressures and government domestic borrowing crowding out
The inverted 15Y-20Y segment suggests limited long-end demand and potential pension/insurance sector absorption constraints, creating reinvestment risk for institutional portfolios
